Container.Name: nebularr
Container.Overview:
Nebularr app (same role as [i]docker-compose.yml[/i] [i]app[/i] service): Web UI, API, metrics, webhooks.
[b]Requires[/b] [i]nebularr-postgres[/i] on the same custom Docker network [b]nebularr[/b] (create the network under Docker → Networks if needed).
[b]DATABASE_URL[/b] defaults match the compose stack: user [i]arrapp[/i] / password [i]arrapp[/i] (created by [i]00_roles.sql[/i]) and host [i]nebularr-postgres[/i]. Change passwords in Postgres init + here if you customize.
[b]Disk:[/b] The Web UI is served from the image; configuration lives in Postgres. The optional path [i]/mnt/user/appdata/nebularr/webserver[/i] → [i]/var/lib/nebularr[/i] is only a reserved host folder (same as Unraid compose); you can remove that mapping if you do not want it.

Config.Name: Web UI / API port
Config.Description: HTTP port for Web UI, REST API, metrics, and webhooks.

Config.Name: App host folder (optional)
Config.Description: Matches Unraid compose: reserved host path; app state is in Postgres, not here.

Config.Name: DATABASE_URL
Config.Description: Must point at your Postgres container hostname on network [i]nebularr[/i].
